definition_origin: Plan selection psychology integrates four established cognitive frameworks into mobile paywall design: (1) the Decoy Effect / asymmetric dominance effect (Huber, Payne & Puto 1982 — Journal of Consumer Research, replicated 100+ times); (2) Default-effect / status-quo bias (Samuelson & Zeckhauser 1988); (3) Anchoring (Tversky & Kahneman 1974); (4) Hick's Law (Hick 1952 — decision time scales logarithmically with choices). Operationalization for mobile paywall design was popularized by RevenueCat Paywalls SDK design templates (2023+), Adapty Paywall Templates Library (2024+), and Superwall paywall design analyses. The 2026-01 Apple Guideline 3.1.2 toggle-paywall ban replaced the toggle pattern with mandatory side-by-side plan cards, making plan-selection psychology the primary lever for paywall conversion in 2026.
core_mechanism: Four levers stacked. (1) DECOY EFFECT — three plans where the middle is bait, not target: Monthly $14.99 (high per-unit cost), Annual $79.99 (target, with 'Most Popular' badge, 56% savings vs monthly), Lifetime $199.99 (decoy that makes Annual look reasonable). Removing Lifetime drops Annual conversion ~15% (industry-standard A/B). (2) DEFAULT HIGHLIGHTING — pre-select Annual with visible outline + checkmark + 'Most Popular' badge. Status-quo bias keeps users on default 60-75% of the time. (3) ANCHORING — show monthly-equivalent of annual ('Just $6.66/mo') and savings ('Save 56% vs monthly') to anchor against the more painful monthly price. LATAM markets respond strongly to this anchoring; US/EU also benefit. (4) HICK'S LAW — 3 plans MAX, 4 if including time-limited (annual + monthly + lifetime + intro). 5+ plans tank conversion via decision paralysis. Post-toggle-ban (2026-01), the SIDE-BY-SIDE CARD layout is mandatory; per-card CTA preferred over single shared CTA.
neuroscience_correlate: Decoy effect engages ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC) — the decoy reframes the value comparison so the target plan becomes the rational-feeling choice. Default-effect engages basal ganglia status-quo circuits. Anchoring engages parietal cortex numerical-comparison pathways (Plassmann 2007, fMRI: anchored prices alter vmPFC value signals 30-40%). Hick's Law: 5+ choices triggers anterior cingulate cortex conflict-monitoring overload → abandonment.
empirical_evidence: Huber, Payne & Puto 1982 (Journal of Consumer Research, replicated 100+ times): adding a decoy product shifts target-product preference 14-25% in choice tasks. Iyengar & Lepper 2000 (Journal of Personality & Social Psychology): jam-tasting study — 6% purchase from 24-jam display vs 30% from 6-jam display (paradox of choice). Kahneman & Tversky 1974: anchoring effects 30-50% in valuation tasks. Adapty 2026 paywall benchmarks (18M+ paywall views, 15B+ events): paywalls with 3 plans convert ~1.35% at onboarding placement; paywalls with 5+ plans convert ~0.6-0.8% (40%+ drop). Default-highlight A/B: pre-selected annual with 'Most Popular' badge converts +18-32% vs no-default per Adapty paywall-experiments-playbook 2026. RevenueCat 2025 Paywalls SDK Template 5 (modal hard paywall) ships with 3-card side-by-side as recommended default. Superwall 2024 paywall analysis (1000+ paywalls): 3-plan side-by-side outperforms 2-plan by 11% conversion when third plan is properly positioned as decoy. Post-toggle-ban (Apple Guideline 3.1.2 effective 2026-01-15): RevenueCat / Adapty rejection-rate data shows side-by-side card layout is the only consistently approved pattern.
measurable_outcome_metric: paywall_conversion_rate (paywall_view → purchase_or_trial), default_plan_selection_rate (% who chose default), plan_card_tap_rate per plan, decision_latency_ms (paywall_view → plan_selected), plan_change_count (how many times user toggles between plans), abandonment_rate per plan_count_variant.
evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + peer_reviewed (decoy / default / anchoring / Hick's Law are peer-reviewed)
conversion_lift_benchmark: +18-32% from default-highlighted plan vs no-default (Adapty 2026). +14-25% target-plan preference shift from decoy plan addition (Huber 1982 replication studies). +11% 3-plan vs 2-plan side-by-side conversion (Superwall 2024). 40%+ conversion drop at 5+ plans vs 3 plans (Adapty 2026 + Iyengar paradox-of-choice generalization).
engineering_tactics: Layout: SIDE-BY-SIDE 3 cards horizontally (or vertically stacked on narrow screens / Dynamic Type 200%). NO TOGGLE (banned 2026-01 by Apple Guideline 3.1.2). Card structure: [Plan name] / [Per-period price prominent (e.g. $79.99/yr 28pt)] / [Per-month equivalent in smaller text ($6.66/mo)] / [Savings badge ('Save 56%' if annual)] / [Default outline + checkmark on target plan] / [Per-card CTA: Start free trial / Subscribe]. Decoy positioning: Lifetime far-right (or far-left); Annual middle with 'Most Popular' badge; Monthly opposite end. Pre-selection logic: const defaultSku = userQuestionnaire.commitment_level === 'long_term' ? 'annual' : 'monthly'; setSelectedPlan(defaultSku). RevenueCat Paywalls SDK Template 5: configure custom_data.plans = [{ id: 'monthly', price: '$14.99/mo' }, { id: 'annual', price: '$79.99/yr', equivalent_price: '$6.66/mo', savings_label: 'Save 56%', is_default: true, badge: 'Most Popular' }, { id: 'lifetime', price: '$199.99' }]. A/B variants: 2-plan vs 3-plan, default-annual vs default-monthly, with-decoy vs without-decoy, savings-badge framing variations ('Save 56%' vs 'vs $14.99/mo'). Telemetry: paywall_plan_selected (sku, was_default, position_index), paywall_plan_changed (from_sku, to_sku) — high plan_change_count signals decision struggle.
real_world_examples: 1) Cal AI (2026-02 post-toggle-ban refactor) — 3-card side-by-side: Monthly $9.99 / Annual $79.99 + 3-day-trial (Most Popular default) / Lifetime $199; lifetime acts as decoy. 2) Headspace — 2-card layout (Annual + Monthly) — represents simpler approach when no decoy is needed; lower conversion vs 3-card per Adapty 2024-2026. 3) Headway — 3-card with Lifetime decoy ($229) making Annual ($89.99) feel reasonable. 4) Duolingo Super — 3-card: Family / Annual (default) / Monthly. 5) Tinder Gold — 3-tier subscription (1mo / 6mo / 12mo) with 6mo as decoy, 12mo as target. 6) Calm — 2-card (Annual default + Lifetime); lifetime as anchor rather than decoy. 7) ChatGPT Plus / Pro — Pro $200/mo positioned as decoy making Plus $20/mo feel reasonable. 8) Notion — 4-card layout (Free / Plus / Business / Enterprise) — exception to Hick's Law via clear B2B segmentation. 9) Spotify — 4-card (Individual / Duo / Family / Student) with student-discount as separate route — uses segmentation to bypass Hick's Law.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: limited horizontal space — 3 cards vertically stacked or 2 cards horizontal + 1 below; iPad/landscape allows true 3-card horizontal. Web: more space — true 3-card horizontal standard. Mobile tap targets ≥44x44pt per card. Mobile A/B easier via remote-config (RevenueCat / Adapty / Superwall server-driven); web A/B requires client-side rendering toggle.
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+): translucent card backgrounds + bold left-aligned plan-name + dynamic morph on selection (card scales to 1.02 + outline). M3 Expressive (Android 16+): expressive shape system differentiates default card via shape token (more pronounced corner radius); 4x faster element discovery (18,000-participant study) makes side-by-side card comparison clearer than legacy toggle. Apple HIG: per-card CTA preferred when card price differs significantly. Material 3: Filled-tonal vs filled vs outlined buttons differentiate primary plan CTA from secondary plan CTAs.
category_fit: Universal — every subscription paywall benefits. STRONG: wellness, AI-wrapper, edtech, fitness, productivity B2B (with 4-card segmentation exception), social (Tinder/Bumble premium tiers). WEAK: pure consumable apps (no recurring plans), one-time-purchase utilities (Adam Lyttle pattern uses single CTA), kids/family with COPPA gating (parental-consent flow precedes plan selection).
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. RevenueCat Paywalls SDK Template 5 or Adapty paywall builder gives the side-by-side card layout out of the box; A/B variants via remote config. Decoy plan addition + default-highlight pre-select = configuration only, no custom UI. Dependencies: RevenueCat (free <$2.5K MTR; Pro $199+/mo), Adapty (free <$10K MRR; Pro $99+/mo), Superwall (free <250 trials/mo).
tooling_options: RevenueCat Paywalls SDK (Template 5 modal, 3-card side-by-side default). Adapty Paywall Builder (drag-drop card layout, AI paywall builder beta). Superwall (campaign rule + paywall A/B). Apphud, Qonversion as alternatives. PostHog feature flags for plan-config A/B (e.g., 2-plan vs 3-plan variant). Mobbin (paywall pattern library for inspiration, 1000+ teardowns).
ai_dependency: none for plan selection logic; optional for personalized SKU recommendation (e.g., 'Based on your goal, Annual recommended' is heuristic, not LLM-required).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a 3-card side-by-side paywall plan-selector in React Native using RevenueCat Paywalls SDK Template 5. Cards horizontal at width >340pt, vertical stack at width <=340pt or Dynamic Type >=150%. Card structure: plan name 18pt bold left, per-period price 28pt bold (e.g. $79.99/yr), monthly-equivalent 14pt grey ($6.66/mo), savings badge for non-Monthly plans (Save 56%). Default plan (Annual) has 2px brand-accent outline + checkmark icon top-right + Most Popular pill badge above card. Per-card CTA below each card (Start free trial / Subscribe / Buy). NO TOGGLE (Apple Guideline 3.1.2 ban). Forbid: indigo-500 outlines, 5+ plans (Hick Law violation), single shared CTA when plans differ in trial-eligibility. Use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Add decoy positioning: Lifetime at far-right ($199.99, no badge, lower visual weight) makes Annual at center (target) feel reasonable. A/B test 2-plan vs 3-plan via PostHog flag. Telemetry: paywall_plan_selected (sku, was_default, position_index, decision_latency_ms).'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate a 3-card side-by-side paywall mockup. Layout: 3 cards equal-width horizontal, 12pt gap. LEFT card Monthly $14.99/mo, no badge, 1pt grey-300 outline. CENTER card Annual $79.99/yr (Most Popular badge: pill-shape brand-accent above card), monthly-equivalent text $6.66/mo Söhne 14pt grey-700 below price, Save 56% badge inline, default-selected with 2px brand-accent outline + checkmark top-right corner, per-card CTA Start free trial 48pt height filled brand-accent. RIGHT card Lifetime $199.99, no badge, 1pt grey-300 outline. Below cards (16pt gap): timeline transparency Söhne 14pt: Today: free for 3 days / Day 4: billed $79.99 / Cancel anytime. Cabinet Grotesk plan names. Forbid: Inter, indigo-500, gradient backgrounds, fake countdowns, 4+ cards, toggle.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the variant for narrow screens (<340pt) or Dynamic Type 200%: same 3 cards but vertically stacked. Default card center remains visually highlighted (outline + checkmark + badge). Per-card CTA below each.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: (1) TOGGLE paywall (BANNED 2026-01 by Apple 3.1.2). (2) 5+ plans (Hick's Law violation, 40% conversion drop). (3) No default plan highlighted. (4) Single shared CTA below all cards (loses per-card clarity post-toggle-ban). (5) Indigo-500 + Lucide icons + Inter font. (6) No monthly-equivalent on annual card (loses anchoring). (7) Decoy plan missing (loses 14-25% target lift). (8) Plans listed in equal visual weight (no decoy positioning). Counter-prompt: '3 plans MAX (Hick Law). Side-by-side cards (NO toggle, banned 2026-01 Apple 3.1.2). Default plan pre-selected with outline + checkmark + Most Popular badge. Per-card CTA. Show monthly-equivalent on annual card for anchoring. Position decoy (Lifetime) at far-end to make Annual feel reasonable. Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope. Forbid Inter, indigo-500, gradients, fake countdowns, toggle.'
anti_patterns: 1) Toggle paywall (BANNED 2026-01). 2) 5+ plans (Hick's Law / Decision Paralysis). 3) No default highlighted (loses 18-32% lift). 4) Decoy missing (loses 14-25% lift). 5) Single shared CTA when plans differ in trial-eligibility (post-toggle-ban requires per-card CTA for clarity). 6) Equal visual weight on all plans (loses default + decoy effects). 7) Hidden savings calculation (8pt grey footnote). 8) Plan ordering randomized (loses position effect — target plan should be CENTER for horizontal, MIDDLE for vertical stack). 9) Monthly-equivalent missing on annual card (loses anchoring). 10) 'Save XX%' calculated against unrealistic baseline (e.g., '$199 vs $0' — DFA fake-discount risk).
regulatory_risk: MEDIUM. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (toggle ban + subscription disclosure). FTC Click-to-Cancel — material plan terms must be 'clear and conspicuous' on single screen. EU DFA Q4 2026 — fake-discount / fake-savings calculations flagged; savings must reference real comparison price. EU Omnibus Directive (2022) — 'lowest price last 30 days' rule for discount displays applies to subscriptions. CA AB 2863 — pre-renewal reminder for trials >$200 lifetime.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.2 (effective 2026-01-15) — TOGGLE BANNED, side-by-side card layout with per-card CTA OR explicit 'Start free trial' CTA with timeline transparency required. Apple StoreKit2 Promotional Offer + Win-back Offer SKUs supported (item 39). Google Play Billing v6 base-plan + offer model (target API 35, Aug 2025). Apple App Store Connect: max 200 in-app subscription SKUs per app (well above the 3-plan recommendation).
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.1 (Use of Color) — default plan must have non-color indicator (outline + checkmark + badge). SC 2.5.8 — touch target ≥24x24px (effective 44x44pt) per card. SC 1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1 on plan name + price + savings badge. Dynamic Type 200% — cards must reflow vertically below ~340pt; never truncate price. VoiceOver: each card announces 'Annual plan, $79.99 per year, $6.66 per month, save 56 percent, includes 3-day free trial, most popular, currently selected' (full announcement). Reduce-motion: replace card-morph animation with instant fade. SC 2.1.1 keyboard accessible — full keyboard nav for hardware-keyboard iPad users.
pitfalls_caveats: 1) Decoy effect can backfire if decoy is too obviously a decoy (users feel manipulated → DFA risk). 2) Default highlighting + Apple's auto-renewing subscription = trap if user doesn't notice default; mitigate with explicit per-card CTA. 3) Savings calculations must reference real comparison price — '$199 vs $0' = fake discount. 4) Position effects: in RTL markets (AR/HE), card order reverses — verify default position remains center. 5) Plan name localization must preserve target-vs-decoy structure (Annual / Lifetime / Monthly relative perception). 6) Trial-eligibility differs per SKU — annual w/trial vs monthly no-trial requires per-card CTA. 7) Family Sharing eligibility flag varies per SKU — surface inline.
cross_cultural_validity: Decoy effect replicated globally but magnitude varies. JP / KR markets: subtler decoy positioning preferred (Lifetime $199 alongside Annual $79.99 reads aggressive in JP — soften visual weight). LATAM: aggressive savings-anchoring works well ('Save 67% vs monthly' shines on annual). DE: BGB §312k pre-renewal reminder copy on default card. CN: 'Most Popular' badge translates as '一番人気' / '最受歡迎'. RTL: full layout mirror including card order.
prescribed_action: Build a 3-card side-by-side paywall (NEVER toggle — banned 2026-01 Apple 3.1.2): Monthly / Annual w/trial (default, Most Popular badge, center position) / Lifetime (decoy, far-end position). Pre-select Annual with 2px outline + checkmark + badge. Show monthly-equivalent on Annual card for anchoring. Per-card CTA for clarity post-toggle-ban. Hard cap 3 plans (Hick's Law). Verify savings calculations reference real comparison prices (DFA / Omnibus Directive). Run A/B variants via RevenueCat Experiments / PostHog: 2 vs 3 plans, default annual vs default monthly, with vs without decoy. Telemetry: paywall_plan_selected with sku + was_default + position_index + decision_latency_ms.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 02_design (card layout + typography + accessibility), 04_implementation (RevenueCat Offering config + per-card CTA wiring), 05_telemetry (plan-selection events).
questionnaire_relevance: Questionnaire identity-questions (commitment-level, goal duration, urgency) seed plan-card pre-selection: 'long-term goal' → Annual default; 'just trying' → Monthly default; 'one-time fix' → Lifetime default. Cross-step personalization stitching (item 14) carries this into paywall card pre-select.
aha_relevance: AI plan reveal (item 12) primes user to want commitment commensurate with revealed plan ambition. Plan selection psychology converts that primed willingness into the SKU choice. If Aha is weak, default highlighting carries more conversion weight; if Aha is strong, decoy effect amplifies target plan further.
telemetry_events: paywall_plan_selected (sku, was_default, position_index, decision_latency_ms), paywall_plan_changed (from_sku, to_sku, switch_count), paywall_plan_card_viewed (sku, scroll_visible_seconds), paywall_plan_card_tapped (sku, position), paywall_savings_label_viewed.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (paywall conversion + default-selection rate), Day 30 (plan-conversion → first-renewal), 12mo cohort LTV per SKU. Decoy effect measurable only in A/B with vs without decoy at the conversion-rate horizon, not LTV.
emotional_archetype_alignment: Sage (informed plan comparison), Ruler (premium tier visibility), Hero (commitment tier signal). Magician (Annual-w/trial as 'unlock transformation' framing). MISMATCH: Innocent (3-plan complexity feels deceptive — 2-plan simpler), Caregiver (decoy effect can feel manipulative — soften with transparent comparison framing), Outlaw (anti-corporate may resent any tiered structure).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01), 5+ Plan Decision Paralysis, Equal-Visual-Weight Plans (loses default + decoy), Fake Discount Anchor (DFA + Omnibus violation), Hidden Material Terms. Aligns with: Hard Paywall Climax (item 34), Trial vs Direct (item 35), Localization (item 36).
sources: [Huber, Payne & Puto 1982 — Decoy Effect (Journal of Consumer Research)](https://www.jstor.org/stable/2488928)
